tasks.title: Add dusty solar panel mechanic with Remove Dust action
tasks.description: Implement the full 'dusty solar panel' feature: dust storms make solar panels dusty (10% energy), visual dust overlay, Remove Dust action with hotkey, tile info display, and logging.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
1. Dust storm tiles overlapping a Solar Panel set it to dusty state.
2. Dusty panels have 10% energy generation (not 50%).
3. Dusty panels are visually distinct (muted colors + dust overlay).
4. 'Remove Dust' action available to any unit on or adjacent to a dusty Solar Panel.
5. Remove Dust restores normal energy production.
6. Remove Dust costs 1 move.
7. Hotkey X assigned and added to hotkey modal.
8. Button shown/hidden per 'Hide Unusable Actions' pattern.
9. Log entries for becoming dusty and for dust removal.
10. Tile info panel shows dusty status.
tasks.claudeMd: # Project Context You are implementing the 'dusty solar panel' feature in a single-file vanilla JS browser game. ## Tech Stack - Pure vanilla JS (game.js ~1730 lines), HTML (index.html), CSS (style.css) - No build system, no framework, no package.json - Canvas-based rendering (2D context) - All game logic in a single IIFE in game.js ## Architecture - `state.structures` array holds structures with `{ type, row, col, ... }` — solar panels are `{ type: 'solar_panel', row, col }` - Add `dusty: false` property to solar panel structures - `state.dustStorms` array holds storms with `{ id, tiles: [{row, col}], direction }` - `checkDustStormCollisions()` (line ~1426) iterates storms and checks overlapping units — add solar panel dustification here - `processSolarPanels()` (line ~1275) uses `Math.random() < 0.5` for energy — change to `< 0.1` when dusty - `drawSolarPanel(structure)` (line ~392) renders panels — add dust overlay when `structure.dusty` - `updateUI()` (line ~760) manages action button visibility using a pattern: array of `[buttonId, canDoAction()]`, buttons get `display: none` when disabled. Follow this exact pattern. - `updateTileInfo()` (line ~830) shows tile details — append '(Dusty)' to Solar Panel label - Hotkey handler is a `switch(e.key)` at line ~1640 — add `case 'x':` - Button click listeners are registered around line ~1586 ## Conventions - Use `var` (not `let/const`) for new function-scoped variables (match existing style in action functions) - Use `function name()` declarations (not arrows) for new functions - Log entries use `addLog(message, type)` — use type `'dust'` for dust events - Action pattern: `canXxx()` returns boolean, `xxx()` performs action with guard `if (!canXxx()) return;` - Button IDs follow pattern: `verb-noun-btn` (e.g., `remove-dust-btn`) - CSS log entry classes: `#game-log .log-entry.dust` ## Implementation Checklist ### 1. game.js — Add dusty property to solar panels - In `buildSolarPanel()` (~line 1000), add `dusty: false` to the structure object - In `checkDustStormCollisions()` (~line 1426), after the unit collision loop for each storm tile, add a structure check: if `getStructureAt(tile.row, tile.col)` is a solar panel and not already dusty, set `dusty = true` and call `addLog(..., 'dust')` ### 2. game.js — Reduce dusty panel energy generation - In `processSolarPanels()` (~line 1282), change `Math.random() < 0.5` to `Math.random() < (panel.dusty ? 0.1 : 0.5)` ### 3. game.js — Draw dusty overlay - In `drawSolarPanel(structure)` (~line 392), after all normal drawing, if `structure.dusty`: - Draw a semi-transparent brown overlay: `ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(139, 90, 43, 0.55)'; ctx.fillRect(x+4, y+8, TILE_SIZE-8, TILE_SIZE-16);` - Draw small dust dots/stipples for visual texture - Change the label color to a muted brown and text to 'DUSTY' ### 4. game.js — canRemoveDust() and removeDust() - `canRemoveDust()`: return true if selected unit has movesLeft > 0 AND there exists a dusty solar panel at the unit's position OR adjacent (use `getStructureAt` for on-tile, `getAdjacentStructures` pattern for adjacent) - `removeDust()`: find the dusty panel (on or adjacent), set `dusty = false`, decrement `unit.movesLeft`, log it, call `refreshView()` - Helper: `findDustySolarPanelNear(row, col)` — checks on-tile first, then adjacent tiles for a dusty solar panel ### 5. index.html — Add button and hotkey row - Add `` after the build-throne-btn - Add `XRemove Dust` in the hotkey table before the Tab row ### 6. game.js — Wire up UI - In `updateUI()`, add `['remove-dust-btn', canRemoveDust()]` to the actions array (before the special Call Earth / Throne handling, around line 789) - Add click listener: `document.getElementById('remove-dust-btn').addEventListener('click', function() { if (state.gameOver) return; removeDust(); });` - Add keyboard case: `case 'x': removeDust(); return;` ### 7. game.js — Tile info - In `updateTileInfo()`, where `tileStructure.type === 'solar_panel'` (~line 847), change to: `structureText = 'Solar Panel' + (tileStructure.dusty ? ' (Dusty)' : '');` ### 8. style.css — Dust log styling - Add: `#game-log .log-entry.dust { color: #b8860b; }` (dark goldenrod, sandy/dusty feel) ## Gotchas - The `checkDustStormCollisions()` function iterates storm tiles that may be off-map (negative coords) — the existing code already has bounds checking (`if (tile.row < 0 || ...`) so your structure check will naturally be inside that guard - Solar panels don't have an `energy` property (only hovels do) — don't confuse the two - The existing `getAdjacentStructures(row, col, type)` returns structures of a specific type adjacent to a position (excludes the position itself). For on-tile, use `getStructureAt(row, col)` separately - When multiple dusty panels are adjacent, `removeDust()` should clean the first one found (on-tile takes priority) - The `init()` function doesn't need changes — new solar panels built after init already get `dusty: false`

checklist.description: Solar panels gain dusty:false property when built and become dusty when overlapped by dust storm
steps: Add dusty: false to the structure object in buildSolarPanel()
In checkDustStormCollisions(), after unit checks per storm tile, check if getStructureAt(tile.row, tile.col) is a non-dusty solar_panel and set dusty=true with log
Verify by reading the code that the dusty flag is set correctly

checklist.description: Dusty solar panels have 10% energy generation instead of 50%
steps: In processSolarPanels(), change Math.random() < 0.5 to Math.random() < (panel.dusty ? 0.1 : 0.5)
Verify the conditional is correctly placed

checklist.description: Dusty solar panels are visually distinct with muted colors and dust overlay
steps: In drawSolarPanel(), after normal rendering, add conditional block for structure.dusty
Draw semi-transparent brown overlay over the panel area
Add dust stipple dots for visual texture
Change label to 'DUSTY' with muted color

checklist.description: canRemoveDust() and removeDust() functions implement the action correctly
steps: Create findDustySolarPanelNear(row, col) helper that checks on-tile then adjacent
Create canRemoveDust() that checks unit.movesLeft > 0 and findDustySolarPanelNear returns a panel
Create removeDust() that sets panel.dusty=false, costs 1 move, logs, and refreshes
Verify removeDust() has guard: if (!canRemoveDust()) return

checklist.description: Remove Dust button in HTML and wired up with click listener
steps: Add <button id='remove-dust-btn'> in index.html action panel
Add click event listener in game.js matching existing pattern
Verify button follows same HTML structure as other action buttons

checklist.description: Remove Dust button shown/hidden following Hide Unusable Actions pattern
steps: Add ['remove-dust-btn', canRemoveDust()] to the actions array in updateUI()
Verify it is included in the anyVisible tracking

checklist.description: Hotkey X triggers Remove Dust and is listed in hotkey modal
steps: Add case 'x': removeDust(); return; in keydown switch
Add XRemove Dust in hotkey table in index.html
Verify placement is before the Tab/Cycle units row

checklist.description: Log entries created for dusty and clean events
steps: Verify addLog call in checkDustStormCollisions when panel becomes dusty uses type 'dust'
Verify addLog call in removeDust() when panel is cleaned uses type 'dust'
Add CSS class #game-log .log-entry.dust with sandy/goldenrod color in style.css

checklist.description: Tile info panel shows dusty status for Solar Panels
steps: In updateTileInfo(), change solar_panel case to append ' (Dusty)' when tileStructure.dusty is true
Verify the info updates correctly for both dusty and clean panels

quality_checklist.description: New code follows existing codebase conventions (var, function declarations, naming patterns)
steps: Check that new functions use var (not let/const) for local variables
Check that new functions are declared with function keyword (not arrows)
Check that button ID follows verb-noun-btn pattern
Check that canXxx/xxx action pattern is followed

quality_checklist.description: No redundant or dead code introduced
steps: Check that findDustySolarPanelNear is used by both canRemoveDust and removeDust (not duplicated logic)
Check no unused variables or imports were added
Check that dusty:false is only added in buildSolarPanel, not redundantly elsewhere

quality_checklist.description: Visual rendering is clean and doesn't break existing solar panel or dust storm rendering
steps: Check that dusty overlay is drawn after normal panel rendering (correct z-order)
Check that dust storm rendering (drawDustStorm) is not affected
Check that the overlay coordinates match the panel area exactly

quality_checklist.description: Edge cases handled: off-map storm tiles, already-dusty panels, no adjacent panels
steps: Check that checkDustStormCollisions bounds-checks before getStructureAt
Check that already-dusty panels don't get double-logged
Check that canRemoveDust returns false when no dusty panel nearby

checks.description: Solar panel becomes dusty when dust storm passes over it
steps: Open app in browser
Build a Rock Hovel and Solar Panel
Wait for a dust storm to pass over the solar panel (or inspect code logic)
Verify the solar panel visually changes to show dust overlay
Verify tile info shows 'Solar Panel (Dusty)' when clicking the panel
Verify a log entry appears about the panel becoming dusty

checks.description: Remove Dust action works and restores panel
steps: With a dusty solar panel, move a unit onto or adjacent to it
Verify 'Remove Dust' button appears and is enabled
Click 'Remove Dust' button
Verify the panel visual returns to normal
Verify unit lost 1 move
Verify log entry about dust removal
Verify tile info no longer shows '(Dusty)'

checks.description: Hotkey X triggers Remove Dust and appears in hotkey modal
steps: Press ESC to open hotkey modal
Verify 'X — Remove Dust' row is present
Close modal, position unit on dusty panel
Press X key
Verify dust is removed

checks.description: Remove Dust button is hidden when not applicable
steps: With no dusty panels nearby, verify Remove Dust button is hidden
With unit having 0 moves left, verify Remove Dust button is hidden even if near dusty panel
